body_txt: A staple of the martial arts genre. Ki (Japanese 気) or Chi/Qi (traditional Chinese 氣, simplified Chinese: 气) commonly translates as "feeling", "atmosphere" or "intent", but in an esoteric context it refers more specifically to a cloudy energy of life force and/or emotions/disposition which circulates through the body (and the world ) like blood. True masters can learn to intensify, diminish or redirect the flow of their ki , resulting in what amounts to superhero-style attacks. Often, rather than simply being called "ki" it will be further qualified to distinguish it from mundane uses of the term, associating it with different kinds of environments or supernatural beings. E.g. Action Series where ki is strongly tied to willpower often call it touki ("fighting spirit"), the cast of a work dealing heavily with the Spirit World might wield reiki ("spirit energy"), and a villainous character might use a distinctively foul form of ki which works like The Dark Side (often called youki , as in Yōkai ki). The more esoteric these get in their applications, the more overlap with Mana is likely to ensue. The original Chinese concept holds that a person has two sets of Ki, a finite set that you're born with and a freely replenishable set that you accumulate over the course of your life. The latter can be increased by meditation, proper diet, martial arts training, etc. When the Ki you were born with runs out, you die. But training yourself to use the replenishable Ki to sustain your body instead and thus conserve the original Ki, you increase your lifespan. If this version of Ki Manipulation is included in a story, it can be used to give an "Asian" flavor to tropes like Wizards Live Longer (specifically, Kung Fu Wizards Live Longer) and Cast from Lifespan . If Functional Magic also exists in the ' Verse , there's usually a distinction made between the two, comparable to the distinction between Psychic Powers and Functional Magic in Western Speculative Fiction . If no distinction is made, it's Supernatural Martial Arts . If a character can use both individually, then they're a Kung-Fu Wizard . At times may overlap with Background Magic Field . Sometimes Ki Manipulation is a part of some Martial Arts and Crafts , making it either Rule of Funny or Rule of Cool . Closely related to Charles Atlas Superpower and Enlightenment Superpower . Commonly wielded by fantastical versions of the Eastern variant of Warrior Monks , and especially Shaolin Monks . Common types of Ki Manipulation include: While sakki ( Killing Intent ) includes the character 気, it is usually treated as a ki in the more mundane sense rather than falling under this trope. May be represented with Blasting Time . Compare Mana , which tends to be more of a scalar than vector quality. See also Hand Blast , an attack which is similar in appearance. The Spirit Cultivation Genre of literature popularized the idea of increasing Ki with training.
